item.ptxt: Before using scan tool, read its Operator’s Manual to know how to use it.
When replacing TCM with used one, all learned gear change conditions which are stored in TCM memory should be cleared after the replacement referring to .
After replacing or reinstalling any one of the following parts, perform .
Auto gear shift actuator unit
Gear shift and select shaft assembly
Clutch or related parts
Manual transaxle or manual transaxle parts (separated from engine)
Engine parts (after separation of manual transaxle from engine)
Read before inspection and observe what is written there.
Communication of TCM and other control module is established by CAN.
For more detail of CAN communication for TCM, refer to .
Therefore, handle CAN communication line with care referring to .

Auto gear shift is a transaxle developed in conjunction with an auto gear shift actuator unit (1), a gear shift and select shaft assembly (2) and a conventional manual transaxle (3) and the clutch pedal is eliminated. Manual transaxle has high mechanical efficiency of power transmission compared with A/T or CVT and, electronically controlled clutch operation and gear shift are optimized for fuel economy almost equivalent to manual transaxle model.

Since the auto gear shift actuator directly operates the clutch release shaft and the gear shift and select shaft assembly, the driver can drive vehicle without clutch operation and changing gears. This automated transaxle has realized smooth shift feeling reducing the deceleration feeling compared with the conventional manual transaxle. In addition, this system has a creep function for easy drive to put the vehicle into a garage or in a traffic jam.

The TCM performs engagement / disengagement of the clutch and gear changes using the auto gear shift actuator by electronic control based on the signals from ECM, BCM and other sensors. Cooperative control between engine and auto gear shift through CAN communication has realized both excellent shift characteristic and low fuel consumption to fit it to the vehicle conditions.

servinfosub.title: Auto Gear Shift Actuator Unit Construction

attention3.ptxt: The component parts of the auto gear shift actuator unit shown below are for explanation.
However, disassembling this unit is not permitted except for the components of the auto gear shift actuator mentioned in this section.

servinfosub.ptxt: The auto gear shift actuator consists of the following devices.

item.ptxt: Actuator harness (1)
Clutch actuator anchor (2)
Clutch actuator boot (3)
Electric motor (4)
Reservoir tank (5)
TCM (6)
Clutch actuator (7)
Clutch position sensor (8)
Valve body (9)
Oil pump (10)
Accumulator (11)

servinfosub.ptxt: The valve body in the auto gear shift actuator is equipped with the following components.

item.ptxt: Select actuator (12)
Select piston (13)
Select position sensor (14)
Shift actuator (15)
Shift piston (16)
Shift position sensor (17)
Pressure sensor (18)
Shift solenoid valve 1 (19)
Shift solenoid valve 2 (20)
Clutch solenoid valve (21)
Select solenoid valve (22)

servinfosub.ptxt: The auto gear shift actuator is operated by the pressure of auto gear shift actuator fluid (hydraulic pressure) generated by the electric oil pump and accumulated in the accumulator.
The hydraulic pressure is monitored by the pressure sensor and, when the pressure becomes lower than the specified value, the electric oil pump is operated.

And, the pressure goes up to the specified value, the electric oil pump is stopped. While the ignition is off, the hydraulic pressure is released and the auto gear shift actuator fluid is returned to the reservoir tank.
TCM supplies the hydraulic pressure to each actuator by controlling shift solenoid No. 1, shift solenoid No. 2, clutch solenoid and select solenoid.

topic.title: Auto gear shift actuator hydraulic circuit
Clutch actuator
topic.ptxt: The clutch actuator consists of a clutch piston and clutch position sensor, and the clutch piston is operated by hydraulic pressure from the clutch solenoid. The operation of the clutch piston turns the clutch release shaft via the clutch release arm and then the clutch release bearing is actuated in push pull directions for disengage / engage of the clutch.

topic.title: Shift actuator
topic.ptxt: The shift actuator consists of a shift piston and shift position sensor, and the shift piston is operated by hydraulic pressure from shift solenoid No. 1 and No. 2. This actuator turns the gear shift shaft in the gear shift and select shaft assembly to shift the gear to 1 &entity-harr; 2 speed, 3 &entity-harr; 4 speed or 5 &entity-harr; R speed.

topic.title: Select actuator
topic.ptxt: The select actuator consists of a select piston and select position sensor, and the select piston is operated by hydraulic pressure from the select solenoid. This actuator moves the gear shift shaft in the gear shift and select shaft assembly up and down to select one of the shift positions in 1 &entity-harr; 2 speed, 3 &entity-harr; 4 speed and 5 &entity-harr; R speed.

servinfosub.title: Auto Gear Shift Transaxle Construction
servinfosub.ptxt: The auto gear shift transaxle provides five forward speeds and one reverse speed by means of three synchromesh devices and three shafts: input shaft, countershaft and reverse gear shaft. All forward gears are in constant mesh, and reverse uses a sliding idler gear arrangement.

entry.ptxt: Low speed synchronizer sleeve and hub
High speed synchronizer sleeve and hub

servinfosub.title: Gear Shift and Clutch Operating Mechanism
servinfosub.ptxt: When the shift selector is operated, the TCM incorporated in auto gear shift actuator unit controls the auto gear shift actuator unit based on the information inputted in it. The clutch solenoid valve causes the clutch to engage / disengage by moving the release shaft, and shift solenoid valve 1, shift solenoid valve 2 and select solenoid valve cause the gears to change by moving the shift fork.

System of the auto gear shift actuator unit to control clutch engagement / disengagement and gear change of the transaxle consists of the shift selector, clutch solenoid valve, shift solenoid valve 1, shift solenoid valve 2, select solenoid valve, VSS, clutch speed sensor, clutch position sensor, shift position sensor, select position sensor, brake light switch, driver’s door switch and TCM and the auto gear shift actuator unit has the following functions.

item.ptxt: Auto gear shift can be driven in D (drive) mode and M (manual) mode.
Operation of shift selector from D to R (reverse) or R to D is not effective if brake pedal is not pressed:
Gear stays in neutral if engine is on at this moment.
Gear does not change if the engine is off & ignition is on at this moment.

If shift up operation by shift selector fails to engage gear to target position, shifting to higher gear is attempted. And, if shift down operation by shift selector fails to engage gear, the gear remains in a position before the shift down.
The engine can be started only when the brake is applied and the shift selector is in N to prevent sudden movement of vehicle.
Gear change is controlled by TCM according to the accelerator operation similarly to automatic transmission and creeping is also available with accelerator and brake pedal released.
Similarly to conventional manual transaxle, it is possible to park the vehicle while the gear is engaged in reverse or 1st position with ignition “OFF”.
The gear is shifted from reverse, D or M to neutral automatically to prevent risk of danger when any one of the following conditions is met with engine running.
During a stop, if driver's door is open with gear shift lever in 'R', 'D' or 'M' position without depressing brake pedal and accelerator pedal for some time.
Specific malfunction is detected.

servinfosub.title: Gear Shift Mechanism
servinfosub.ptxt: When shift selector is operated, TCM controls shift solenoid No. 1, shift solenoid No. 2 and select solenoid based on each input signal. And, hydraulic pressure accumulated in accumulator (1) operates shift piston (2) in the shift actuator and select piston (3) in the select actuator. The shift piston turns the gear shift shaft in “a” directions (1 &entity-harr; 2 speed, 3 &entity-harr; 4 speed or 5 &entity-harr; R speed) to shift the gear, and the select piston changes the select position in “b” direction (1 &entity-harr; 2 speed, 3 &entity-harr; 4 speed or 5 &entity-harr; R speed).

entry.ptxt: Auto gear shift actuator unit viewed from bottom
Gear shift and select shaft assembly

servinfosub.title: Clutch Operating Mechanism
servinfosub.ptxt: When the vehicle is stopped with the engine off, the clutch is engaged like a conventional manual transaxle.

And, when the engine is started, TCM starts up the clutch solenoid based on each input signal. And the hydraulic pressure in the accumulator actuates clutch piston (1) in clutch actuator (2) to pull the clutch release arm (3). The pulling extent of clutch release arm has been learned by TCM sensing the contact point between clutch release arm and stopper for the arm on transaxle rib. According to this movement of clutch release arm, clutch release bearing (4) is pushed out to press down clutch diaphragm spring and then the diaphragm spring separates pressure plate from the flywheel and driving force from the engine is cut off.

In addition, creep function and half clutch function are controlled changing conducting ratio to the clutch solenoid. While the vehicle is stopped with the parking lever pulled or brake pedal depressed, this creep function is disabled for protection of the clutch disk. However, when the parking lever and brake pedal are released, the clutch is engaged gradually and driving force is generated.

servinfosub.title: Warning Buzzer Operation
servinfosub.ptxt: The combination meter sounds the warning buzzer in two patterns as follows.

entry.ptxt: When gear is disengaged and shifted to neutral automatically for safety reasons.
When shifting to reverse gear is inhibited due to vehicle condition.
When cranking without depressing brake pedal.
While the vehicle is still moving, shift selector is shifted to D, M or R without depressing brake pedal. (garage shift)
When shift selector position and actual gear position disagree.

servinfosub.title: Depress Brake Pedal Indicator Operation
servinfosub.ptxt: When any of the following operations is executed, depress brake pedal indicator (1) comes ON to warn the driver.

item.ptxt: While the vehicle is still moving, shift selector is shifted to D, M or R without depressing brake pedal. (garage shift)
When cranking without depressing brake pedal.

servinfo.title: Function of Auto Gear Shift Control System Component

entry.ptxt: Detects oil pressure in auto gear shift actuator unit.
Opens / closes oil circuit for moving clutch actuator.
Opens / closes oil circuit for moving engage piston.
Opens / closes oil circuit for moving select piston.
Operated by clutch solenoid valve for clutch engagement / disengagement.
Moves gear shift and select shaft assembly according to shift solenoid valves 1 and 2.
Moves gear shift and select shaft assembly according to select solenoid valve.
Operates oil pump by demand from TCM.
Generates oil pressure in auto gear shift actuator unit.
Accumulates oil pressure generated by oil pump.

item.ptxt: Controls gear shift and clutch operation.
Diagnoses auto gear shift control system components.

entry.ptxt: Gear shift and select shaft assembly
Transmits movement of engage piston and select piston in auto gear shift actuator unit to gear shift shaft.

servinfo.title: Automatic Gear Shift Table
servinfo.ptxt: Automatic gear shift schedule is shown in the following table. Road test the vehicle on a flat road in D range.

ptxt.emph: UP shift

entry.ptxt: There is no sudden change in accelerator opening.
Hard brake is not being applied.

ptxt.emph: DOWN shift

entry.ptxt: There is no sudden change in accelerator opening.
Hard brake is not being applied.

attention3.ptxt: The gear change is done at the shift point different from the above if any of the following conditions is met. Bear this in mind when performing inspection.

servinfo.title: Auto Gear Shift Control System Description
servinfo.ptxt: When the driver's door is opened regardless of ignition ON / OFF, electric oil pump is driven to raise the hydraulic pressure if it is judged that the pressure accumulated in the accumulator is lower than the specified value according to the signal from the pressure sensor. Through this pressure rising, smooth operation of the shift / select and clutch actuator is realized right after the ignition ON.

servinfosub.title: Cranking Control
servinfosub.ptxt: The starting motor control relay operates only at the time the brake pedal is depressed (stop light switch: ON) and the shift selector is in N range. Therefore, unintended starting out is prevented. Further, if ignition switch is turned to start position without depressing the brake pedal, the brake request indicator in the combination meter is lit and the warning buzzer sounds to urge the driver to depress the brake pedal.

servinfosub.title: Shift Control
servinfosub.ptxt: TCM controls the shift solenoid No. 1, No. 2 and the select solenoid for optimal gear shift corresponding to the engine torque based on information of accelerator opening, engine speed and torque.

topic.title: Learning function
topic.ptxt: Assembled parts in the transaxle such as shift forks, gears, etc. and gear shift and select shaft assembly change engagement positions of the respective gears caused by aging and manufacturing tolerance. In order to compensate these factors and keep the smooth shifting, TCM provides the following gear position learning functions.

item.ptxt: Shift actuator position at engaged condition in each gear (1 &entity-harr; 2 speed, 3 &entity-harr; 4 speed and 5 &entity-harr; R speed).
Shift actuator position in neutral.
Select actuator position at selected condition in each speed range (1 &entity-harr; 2 speed, 3 &entity-harr; 4 speed and 5 &entity-harr; R speed).

topic.title: D (Drive) mode control
topic.ptxt: When the shift selector is in D range, shift solenoid No. 1, No. 2 and select solenoid are controlled for optimal gear change. The solenoid valves are controlled based on the programmed gear shift timing and adjusting it by judging driving conditions according to the throttle opening informed from ECM.

topic.title: M (Manual) mode control
topic.ptxt: When the shift selector is in “M”, “+” (shift up) or “-” (shift down) can be used for selecting gear position and the driver can drive the vehicle in the desired gear position.
The selected gear position is displayed on the information display in the combination meter.
Since the M (manual) mode is selected by driver’s intention, the gear position is fixed in “M”. Therefore, change to the D (drive) mode is not performed automatically.

attention3.ptxt: Even if the TCM receives the shift request, if the condition such as throttle opening, engine speed and vehicle speed deviate from the specified range, the shift may not be executed in some cases.

ptxt.emph: Up shift

topic.ptxt: During M (manual) mode control, TCM executes shift up when shifting up is operated by shift selector. However, if the clutch rotational speed after shifting is less than the specified value, the upshifting is canceled in order to prevent the engine stall.

ptxt.emph: Down shift

topic.ptxt: During the M (manual) mode control, if the shift selector is operated to shift down, TCM executes shift down. However, if the clutch rotational speed after shifting is over the specified value, shift down is canceled for the engine protection.

topic.title: Kickdown control (in D (drive) mode)
topic.ptxt: When the accelerator pedal is depressed more than the specified value, TCM judges that the vehicle is in acceleration state and performs a down shift to improve acceleration performance.

topic.title: Under speed control (in D (drive) mode and M (manual) mode)
topic.ptxt: If the vehicle is running at low speed with a high gear position like 4th gear or 5th gear, TCM executes a down shift judging that driving torque is insufficient receiving information such as the gear position and the engine speed. This prevents engine stall. However, when down shifting in M (manual) mode, the gear position is fixed even if the vehicle speed increases.

topic.title: Shift hold control when accelerator (throttle) is quickly closed
topic.ptxt: When the accelerator is closed during acceleration and the accelerator is depressed again, up shift is executed usually because the gear change point for accelerator 0% opening is used as a reference value. However, this shift hold control prevents unnecessary up shift holding the gear in the position when the accelerator pedal is released. In this way, the engine brake can be used effectively and acceleration after that will be done smoothly.

topic.title: Shift hold control using estimated gradient of slope
topic.ptxt: While the vehicle is running, the gradient of the road surface is constantly estimated based on the engine torque information from ECM and the vehicle speed signal. When it is judged that the estimated slope exceeds the specified value, unnecessary shift change is avoided. As a result, the engine brake can be used on the down-hill slope and suitable gear position is maintained on the up-hill slope.

topic.title: Coast down control when brake is applied
topic.ptxt: When coasting of the vehicle is judged based on the brake signal and vehicle deceleration (calculated from the vehicle speed signal), this control changes the normal gear position to the gear position for the coasting. Consequently, at hard braking, down shift is executed at a higher vehicle speed than the normal down shift vehicle speed and is over during deceleration. In this way, time lag at the time of re-acceleration (without this control, down shift takes place after the accelerator pedal is depressed) is eliminated for smooth acceleration.

servinfosub.title: Clutch Control
servinfosub.ptxt: TCM controls the clutch solenoid based on clutch engagement characteristic programmed in advance and judging the vehicle running conditions and the operation state of the driver using the accelerator opening information from the ECM and the vehicle speed signal.

topic.title: Learning function
topic.ptxt: The clutch disc changes engagement point and torque transmission characteristic caused by aging and temperature of the disc. For smooth clutch engagement at the gear change compensating the characteristic changes, TCM has the learning function for the following factors.

item.ptxt: Clutch engagement point: The point where the clutch disk and the flywheel are fully engaged.
Clutch torque transmission point: The point where the clutch disc and the flywheel begin to contact and the engine torque starts to be transmitted to the transmission.
Clutch torque characteristic: Index corresponding to wear and temperature of the clutch disc.
Clutch end stroke: The point where the clutch disc and the flywheel are completely disengaged.

topic.title: Vehicle start control
topic.ptxt: For the vehicle start, shift solenoid No. 1, No. 2 and clutch solenoid are duty controlled on the basis of accelerator opening. And, to realize smooth start, the shift speed and the engagement speed of the clutch are adjusted in response to the vehicle acceleration and deceleration.

topic.title: Creep control
topic.ptxt: For creep function, shift solenoid No. 1, No. 2 and the clutch solenoid are duty controlled by the TCM when the accelerator is closed and shift selector is in D, M or R range. In this condition, the vehicle can run at the set speed.

topic.title: Clutch protection control
topic.ptxt: When estimated temperature of the clutch disc reaches about 200 &entity-deg;C (392 &entity-deg;F), TCM sounds warning buzzer and blinks transmission warning light to warn the driver. This prevents the clutch temperature from rising to the temperature at which the clutch generates odor due to high temperature. In this condition, if the vehicle is driven continuously, the clutch generates odor due to high temperature. Further, if estimated temperature of the clutch disc reaches about 250 &entity-deg;C (482 &entity-deg;F), sounding warning buzzer and blinking transmission warning light still continue and creep function is disabled for protection of clutch disk and clutch slipping for vehicle start is decreased. When this clutch protection control is activated, the driver is required to stop the vehicle at a safe place in order to cool the clutch and carry out cooling of the clutch disc by stopping the engine, or run the vehicle on a flat road at 10 km/h (6 mile/h) or more.

servinfosub.title: TCM Learning
servinfosub.ptxt: The TCM monitors and learns conditions of the clutch and manual transaxle for optimum gear change. However, if clutch, gear shift and select shaft assembly, auto gear shift actuator unit or manual transaxle is removed or replaced, the learned values stored in the TCM deviate from the actual state. Therefore, it is necessary to clear the learned value in the past using SUZUKI scan tool when removing or replacing these parts and make the TCM initialize and learn necessary values again for optimum control of the clutch and transaxle. For the initialization, refer to .

servinfo.title: Auto Gear Shift Diagnosis General Description
servinfo.ptxt: TCM has an OBD System which detects a malfunction in this system.
When diagnosing a trouble in auto gear shift transaxle control including this system, have full understanding of and each item in and execute diagnosis according to to obtain correct result smoothly.

item.ptxt: When TCM detects a malfunction in auto gear shift control system, TCM turns on MIL (1) and/or auto gear shift warning light (2) and stores malfunction DTC in TCM memory.
It is possible to communicate with TCM through DLC (3) using SUZUKI scan tool (4). (Diagnostic information can be checked and cleared using SUZUKI scan tool.)
For information about warm-up cycle, driving cycle and freeze frame data, refer to “OBD System Description”.

servinfosub.title: Warm-Up Cycle
servinfosub.ptxt: A warm-).up cycle means sufficient vehicle operation such that the coolant temperature has risen by at least 22 &entity-deg;C (40 &entity-deg;F) from engine starting and reaches a minimum temperature of 70 &entity-deg;C (160 &entity-deg;F

servinfosub.title: Driving Cycle
servinfosub.ptxt: A “Driving Cycle” consists of engine startup, driving mode where a malfunction would be detected if present, and engine shutoff.

servinfo.title: CAN Communication System Description
servinfo.ptxt: Refer to for CAN communication system description.

testgroup.question: Is there any problem symptom, DTC or abnormal condition?

servinfosub.title: Step 1: Customer Complaint Analysis
servinfosub.ptxt: Record details of the problem (failure, complaint) and how it occurred by the customer.
For this purpose, use of such a questionnaire from shown below will facilitate collecting information for proper analysis and diagnosis.

servinfosub.title: Step 2: DTC / Freeze Frame Data Check, Record and Clearance
servinfosub.ptxt: First, check DTC (current DTC).
If DTC is indicated, print DTC and freeze frame data or write them down and then clear them.

servinfosub.title: Step 3 and 4: Visual Inspection
servinfosub.ptxt: As a preliminary step, perform visual check of the items that support proper function of the engine and auto gear shift referring to .

servinfosub.title: Step 5: Trouble Symptom Confirmation
servinfosub.ptxt: Recheck DTC according to “DTC Confirmation Procedure” described in each DTC troubleshooting.

servinfosub.title: Step 6 and 7: Rechecking and Record of DTC and Freeze Frame Data
servinfosub.ptxt: Refer to for checking procedure.

servinfosub.title: Step 8: Auto Gear Shift Symptom Diagnosis
servinfosub.ptxt: Check the parts of the system suspected as a possible cause referring to .

servinfosub.title: Step 9: Troubleshooting for DTC
servinfosub.ptxt: Perform applicable DTC troubleshooting and repair or replace faulty parts.

servinfosub.title: Step 10: Intermittent Problem Check
servinfosub.ptxt: Check parts related to detected DTC (e.g. wire harness, connector, etc.).

servinfosub.title: Step 11: Final Confirmation Test
servinfosub.ptxt: Check that the problem symptom has gone and the vehicle is free from any abnormal conditions. If what has been repaired is related to DTC, clear DTC once, perform DTC confirmation procedure and check that no DTC is indicated.

servinfo.title: Visual Inspection
servinfo.ptxt: Visually check the following parts and systems.

item.ptxt: Auto gear shift transaxle oil – level, leakage
Engine mountings – play, looseness, damage
Suspension – play, looseness
Drive shafts – damage
Battery – indicator condition, corrosion of terminal
Connectors of electric wire harness – disconnection, friction
Fuses – burning
Parts – installation, damage
Bolts – looseness
Other parts that can be checked visually

entry.ptxt: Also check the following items at engine start, if possible.

item.ptxt: Auto gear shift warning light – Operation
MIL – Operation
Charging light – Operation
Other parts that can be checked visually

servinfo.title: Auto Gear Shift Warning Light Check

s1.ptxt: Check that auto gear shift warning light (1) lights for about 2 sec. and then goes OFF. If anything faulty is found, refer to or .

attention3.ptxt: In case that MIL turns on, the malfunction(s) is detected in either ECM or TCM and it stores the trouble information (DTC, freeze frame data, etc.) in its memory.
According to the varieties of scan tool, check the trouble information as follows.

item.ptxt: SUZUKI scan tool:
It is necessary to check both ECM and TCM.
CAN communication OBD-II generic scan tool:
It is not necessary to check both ECM and TCM separately because the trouble information of both modules can be read and displayed at once.

s1.ptxt: Connect SUZUKI scan tool to DLC (1).
Read DTC and freeze frame data according to instructions displayed on SUZUKI scan tool and print them or write them down. Refer to Operator's Manual of SUZUKI scan tool for further details.

attention3.ptxt: If communication between SUZUKI scan tool and TCM is not possible, perform the following checks:

item.ptxt: Check communication condition between PC and VCI (2) using SUZUKI scan tool function.
Check DLC power circuit and ground circuit.
Check DLC cable. If necessary, check DLC cable by substituting a known-good DLC cable.
Check CAN communication system. (“Control Module Communication Bus Off” is detected in any control module.)
Check TCM power circuit and ground circuit.

s1.ptxt: After completing the check, set ignition “OFF” and disconnect SUZUKI scan tool from DLC.
Connect SUZUKI scan tool to DLC (1).
Clear DTC according to instructions displayed on SUZUKI scan tool. Refer to Operator's Manual of SUZUKI scan tool for further details.

attention3.ptxt: If communication between SUZUKI scan tool and TCM is not possible, perform the following checks:

item.ptxt: Check communication condition between PC and VCI (2) using SUZUKI scan tool function.
Check DLC power circuit and ground circuit.
Check DLC cable. If necessary, check DLC cable by substituting a known-good DLC cable.
Check CAN communication system. (“Control Module Communication Bus Off” is detected in any control module.)
Check TCM power circuit and ground circuit.

s1.ptxt: After completing DTC clearance, set ignition “OFF” and disconnect SUZUKI scan tool from DLC.

attention3.ptxt: Confirmed DTC and freeze frame data stored in TCM memory are also cleared in the following cases. Be careful not to clear them before recording them.

item.ptxt: When the same malfunction (DTC) is not detected again for 40 engine warm-up cycles.
Even though the pending and confirmed DTCs are cleared, the permanent DTC will not be cleared.
DTC with in the following table cannot be read with CAN communication OBD-II generic scan tool.
For details of D/C (driving cycle), refer to .
Two or more DTCs of P188A, P188B, P188C, P188D, P188E or P188F could be detected simultaneously.

item.ptxt: Power supply voltage for pressure sensor, clutch position sensor and clutch speed sensor is higher than specified value.
Power supply voltage for pressure sensor, clutch position sensor and clutch speed sensor is lower than specified value.

entry.ptxt: If any one of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Power supply voltage for shift position sensor and select position sensor is higher than specified value.
Power supply voltage for shift position sensor and select position sensor is lower than specified value.

entry.ptxt: Power supply voltage for solenoid drivers is lower than specified value.
More than specified time has passed after power load relay for solenoid drivers is OFF, power supply voltage for solenoid drivers is higher than specified value.
Clutch-end-stroke-position learning (recognition of clutch stopper position) is not completed.
Clutch-stroke learning (recognition of distance between clutch closed position and clutch stopper) is not possible.
If any one of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Feedback electric current of electric motor is out of specified range.
Feedback voltage of electric motor is out of specified range.
Oil pressure is higher than specified value without operation of oil pump.

entry.ptxt: TCM internal failure (safety function control error)
MMP failure is detected by SMP in TCM.
Malfunction of back up light relay and its circuit (open, shorted to power supply or shorted to ground) is detected.
Driver side front door switch signal is not inputted for specified time.
Malfunction of clutch solenoid valve is detected.
Malfunction of select solenoid valve is detected.
Transmission Range Sensor Circuit Fault Type “A”
None of shift selector position sensor signals is inputted.
Transmission Range Sensor Circuit Fault Type “B”
None of M1, M2, M3, M4 and M5 sensor signals is inputted.
Transmission Range Sensor Circuit Fault Type “C”
One of M1, M2, H1, H2 or H3 sensor signals is not inputted or abnormal.
Transmission Range Sensor Circuit Fault Type “D”
M3, M4 and M5 shift selector position sensor signals are not inputted.
Transmission Range Sensor Circuit Fault Type “E”
One of M3, M4, M5 or H2 sensor signals is not inputted or abnormal.
Transmission Range Sensor Circuit Fault Type “F”
Failure of shift selector position sensor signals except P188A, P188B, P188C, P188D and P188E
Request to shift selector power supply voltage and feedback signal disagree.
Clutch Not Opening Before Gear Shifting
Clutch is not released before gear shifting.
Clutch is engaged during gear shifting.
If any one of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Clutch is engaged suddenly.
Clutch sticks at disengaged state.

entry.ptxt: Gear is shifted to unintended position.
Gear is disengaged without request of disengagement.
Gear is engaged without gear shift request.
Gear shift is not completed within specified time.
Clutch speed exceeds specified value while gear is engaged.
Oil pump temperature exceeds specified value.
Accumulated oil volume exceeds specified limit.
If the following conditions are met.

item.ptxt: The number of gear shift exceeds specified value during specified period of time.
Oil pump temperature exceeds specified value.

entry.ptxt: CAN Invalid Data of Engine Torque
Internal Control Module Monitoring Processor Output Failure
TCM internal failure (monitoring processor output failure)
TCM internal failure (safety input preprocessing control error)
TCM detects a condition which is not acceptable for engine torque up in ECM.
Vehicle equipment information self-learning not completed
TCM does not complete vehicle equipment information self-learning.
Vehicle equipment information does not match to vehicle equipment information self-learning results stored into TCM.
If any one of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Oil leakage exceeds specified value while oil pump is not operating.
Oil leakage exceeds specified value while oil pump is operating.

entry.ptxt: If any one of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Clutch closed position is more than specified value.
Clutch closed position is less than specified value.

entry.ptxt: Lost Communication With ABS/ESP&entity-reg; Control Module
Lost Communication With Body Control Module
Lost Communication With Instrument Panel Cluster (IPC) Control Module

servinfo.title: Fail-Safe Table
servinfo.ptxt: TCM has fail-safe function that assures minimum driving performance even when the control system fails.
When any of the following DTC(s) is detected, TCM control enters the fail-safe mode as long as the failure condition associated with the DTC continues.

entry.ptxt: TCM functions assuming that ambient temperature is 20 °C (68 °F).
TCM shifts gear to neutral if the following conditions are met while engine is running.

item.ptxt: Vehicle is stopped.
Accelerator pedal is released.
Gear is engaged.
Brake pedal is released.

entry.ptxt: Auto gear shift actuator unit cannot shift gear to other than neutral.

item.ptxt: TCM inhibits creep control.
TCM substitutes brake switch (control signal) for brake light switch (brake light signal).

item.ptxt: TCM stops gear shift control.
TCM stops cranking.

TCM substitutes engine speed sensor signal or clutch speed sensor signal for vehicle speed by calculation.

scantool.ptxt: As the data values given in the following table are standard values estimated on the basis of values obtained from the normally operating vehicles using a scan tool, use them as reference values. Even when the vehicle is in good condition, there may be cases where the checked value does not fall within each specified data range. Therefore, judgment as abnormal should not be made depending on these data alone.

Also, condition in the following table that can be checked by the scan tool are those detected by TCM and output from TCM as commands and there may be cases where the auto gear shift or actuator is not operating (in the condition) as indicated by the scan tool.

item.ptxt: If communication between SUZUKI scan tool and TCM is not possible, perform the following checks:
Check communication condition between PC and VCI using SUZUKI scan tool function.
Check DLC power circuit and ground circuit.
Check DLC cable. If necessary, check DLC cable by substituting a known-good DLC cable.
Check CAN communication system. (“Control Module Communication Bus Off” is detected in any control module.)
Check TCM power circuit and ground circuit.
The following scan tool data related to auto gear shift can be checked only by communicating with TCM.
Vehicle: Stop
Shift selector: R, D or M range

topic.title: Input Shaft Revolution (rpm)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates input shaft speed detected by clutch speed sensor.

topic.title: Output Shaft Revolution (rpm)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates output shaft speed calculated by multiplying input shaft speed and reduction gear ratio.

topic.title: Clutch Position Sensor
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of clutch position sensor.

topic.title: Select Position Sensor
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of select position sensor.

topic.title: Shift Position Sensor
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of shift position sensor.

topic.title: Battery Voltage (V)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates battery positive voltage inputted to TCM.

topic.title: Oil Pressure (MPa)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates oil pressure detected by pressure sensor.

topic.title: Clutch Solenoid Control Value (mA)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates control current value that TCM outputs to clutch solenoid valve.

topic.title: Select Solenoid Control Value (mA)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates control current value that TCM outputs to select solenoid valve.

topic.title: Shift Solenoid No.1 (Odd Side) Control Value (mA)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates control current value that TCM outputs to shift solenoid valve 1.

topic.title: Shift Solenoid No.2 (Even Side) Control Value (mA)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates control current value that TCM outputs to shift solenoid valve 2.

topic.title: Engine Speed (rpm)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates engine speed sent from ECM through CAN.

topic.title: Throttle Position (%)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates throttle position sent from ECM through CAN.

topic.title: Accel Effective Position (Accelerator Effective Position (%))
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates accelerator pedal position sent from ECM through CAN.

topic.title: ECT (Engine Coolant Temperature, &entity-deg;C, &entity-deg;F)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates engine coolant temperature sent from ECM through CAN.

topic.title: Outside Air Temp (Outside Air Temperature, °C, °F)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates outside air temperature sent from BCM through CAN.

topic.title: Barometric Pressure (kPa)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates barometric pressure sent from ECM through CAN.

topic.title: Vehicle Speed (km/h)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates vehicle speed detected by VSS.

topic.title: Clutch Control Status
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of clutch control. Closed: Shift selector is in N with ignition “ON” and engine stopped.
Slipping Before Engaging: Shift selector is being shifted from N to D with ignition “ON”.
Slipping After Engaging: After completion of N to D shift with ignition “ON”
Opened: Shift selector is in N with engine idling.

topic.title: Gear Position
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates shift position information in drive mode.

topic.title: Manual Gear Position
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates shift position information in manual mode.

topic.title: Estimated Clutch Temperature (&entity-deg;C, &entity-deg;F)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates estimated clutch temperature calculated by TCM.

topic.title: Clutch High Temperature Control Status
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of clutch high temperature control.
Normal: Estimated clutch temperature is less than 200 &entity-deg;C (392 &entity-deg;F).
Hot Mode 1: Estimated clutch temperature is higher than 200 &entity-deg;C (392 &entity-deg;F).
Hot Mode 2: Estimated clutch temperature is higher than hot mode 1.

topic.title: Automatic Discrimination (ABS/ESP)
topic.ptxt: This parameter is indicated all the time without change.

topic.title: ABS Equipped
topic.ptxt: This parameter is indicated all the time without change.

topic.title: ESP / ABS+Hill Hold Equipped
topic.ptxt: This parameter is indicated all the time without change.

topic.title: Automatic Discrimination (AEB)
topic.ptxt: This parameter is indicated all the time without change.

topic.title: AEB Equipped
topic.ptxt: This parameter is indicated all the time without change.

topic.title: Automatic Discrimination (Push Start)
topic.ptxt: This parameter is indicated all the time without change.

topic.title: Push Start System Equipped
topic.ptxt: This parameter is indicated all the time without change.

topic.title: Lever Position Switch: M1 (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of R or N range sensor in shift selector.
ON: Shift selector is in R or N.
OFF: Shift selector is in D, M, + or –.

topic.title: Lever Position Switch: M2 (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of N, D, M, + or – range sensor in shift selector.
ON: Shift selector is in N, D, M, + or –.
OFF: Shift selector is in R.

topic.title: Lever Position Switch: M3 (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of M, + or – range sensor in shift selector.
ON: Shift selector is in M, + or –.
OFF: Shift selector is in R, N or D.

topic.title: Lever Position Switch: M4 (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of R, N, D, M or + range sensor in shift selector.
ON: Shift selector is in R, N, D, M or +.
OFF: Shift selector is in –.

topic.title: Lever Position Switch: M5 (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of R, N, D, M or – range sensor in shift selector.
ON: Shift selector is in R, N, D, M or –.
OFF: Shift selector is in +.

topic.title: Lever Position Switch: H1 (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of reverse sensor in shift selector.
ON: Shift selector is in R.
OFF: Shift selector is in N, D, M, + or –.

topic.title: Lever Position Switch: H2 (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of neutral sensor in shift selector.
ON: Shift selector is in N.
OFF: Shift selector is in R, D, M, + or –.

topic.title: Lever Position Switch: H3 (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of forward sensor in shift selector.
ON: Shift selector is in D, M, + or –.
OFF: Shift selector is in R or N.

topic.title: Driv Door SW (Wire Harness) (Driver’s Door Switch Wiring Harness) (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates driver’s door switch wiring harness.
ON: Driver's door is open.
OFF: Driver's door is closed.

topic.title: Brake Pedal Switch No.1 (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates brake light switch inputted to TCM.
ON: Brake light switch is ON. (Brake pedal depressed)
OFF: Brake light switch is OFF. (Brake pedal released)

topic.title: Brake Pedal Switch No.2 (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates brake switch inputted to TCM.
ON: Brake switch is ON. (Brake pedal released)
OFF: Brake switch is OFF. (Brake pedal depressed)

topic.title: Hydraulic Pump Active (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of hydraulic pump.
ON: Hydraulic pump operating
OFF: Hydraulic pump not operating

topic.title: Driv Door SW (CAN) (Driver’s Door Switch (CAN))
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates driver’s door switch sent from BCM through CAN.
Open: Driver's door is open.
Close: Driver's door is closed.

topic.title: Parking Brake SW (Parking Brake Switch) (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates parking brake switch sent from BCM through CAN.
ON: Parking brake lever is pulled.
OFF: Parking brake lever is released.

topic.title: A/C Comp Clutch (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates air conditioner request information received from ECM through CAN.
ON: A/C request signal is ON.
OFF: A/C request signal is OFF.

topic.title: Fuel Cut Active (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the state of fuel cut signal from ECM through CAN.
ON: Fuel injection is cut (output signal to injector is stopped).
OFF: Fuel injection is not cut.

topic.title: Hill Hold Control Active (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates all the time without change.

topic.title: Transaxle Warning Light (Lighting) (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of auto gear shift warning light (lighting) output signal from TCM through CAN.
ON: Engine is running with faulty condition.
OFF: Engine is running in normal condition.

topic.title: Transaxle Warning Light (Flashing) (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of auto gear shift warning light (flashing) output signal from TCM through CAN.
ON: Clutch is overheating.
OFF: Clutch temperature is normal.

topic.title: MIL Request (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of MIL output signal from TCM through CAN.
ON: TCM requests combination meter to turn on MIL.
OFF: TCM does not request combination meter to turn on MIL.

topic.title: Creep Control Active (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of creep control.
ON: Creep control operating
OFF: Creep control not operating

topic.title: Driv Door SW (Driver’s Door Switch) (ON/OFF)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates driver’s door switch inputted to TCM.
ON: Driver's door is open.
OFF: Driver's door is closed.

topic.title: A/M State (Automatic / Manual State)
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the gear shifting mode. Automatic: Automatic gear shifting
Manual: Manual gear shifting

topic.title: Contact Point Learning Complete
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of contact-point learning.
Complete: Contact-point learning is completed.
Incomplete: Contact-point learning is not completed.

topic.title: Clutch Close Learning Complete
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of clutch close learning.
Complete: Clutch close learning is completed.
Incomplete: Clutch close learning is not completed.

topic.title: Clutch End Stroke Learning Complete
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of clutch end stroke learning.
Complete: Clutch end stroke learning is completed.
Incomplete: Clutch end stroke learning is not completed.

topic.title: Clutch Stroke Check Complete
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of clutch stroke check.
Complete: Clutch-stroke learning is completed.
Incomplete: Clutch-stroke learning is not completed.

topic.title: Grid Learning Complete
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the states of grid learning.
Complete: Grid learning is completed.
Incomplete: Grid learning is not completed.

topic.title: Transmission Gear Selector Position
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates shift position information.

servinfo.title: MIL Does Not Come ON with Ignition “ON” and Engine Stopped (but Engine Can Be Started)
servinfo.ptxt: Refer to “MIL Does Not Come ON with Ignition “ON” and Engine Stopped (but Engine Can Be Started)” in Section 1A.

servinfo.title: MIL Remains ON after Engine Starts
Auto Gear Shift Warning Light Does Not Come ON at Ignition “ON” and Engine Stopped

test1.ptxt: Check DTC related to CAN communication.

action.ptxt: Go to troubleshooting for applicable DTC.

test1.ptxt: Check combination meter.

action.ptxt: Replace auto gear shift actuator unit and recheck DTC.

servinfo.title: Auto Gear Shift Warning Light Remains ON after Engine Starts

test1.ptxt: Check DTC.

action.ptxt: Go to troubleshooting for applicable DTC.
Replace combination meter and recheck DTC.
If auto gear shift warning light still remains ON, replace auto gear shift actuator unit and recheck DTC.

servinfo.title: Starting Motor Control Relay Circuit Check

entry.ptxt: (Starting motor control relay power supply (switch side) circuit)

test1.ptxt: Check DTC.

action.ptxt: Go to troubleshooting for applicable DTC.

testgroup.title: Starting motor control relay power supply (switch side) check

test1.ptxt: With ignition “OFF”, remove starting motor control relay.
Check for proper terminal connection to starting motor control relay connector.
If connection is OK, check that voltage between “A1” and ground is battery voltage.

testgroup.question: Is check result OK?

action.ptxt: Repair or replace defective wire harness.

testgroup.title: Starting motor control relay signal circuit check

test1.ptxt: Disconnect starting motor connector.
Check for proper terminal connection to starting motor connector.
If connection is OK, check the following points.

item.ptxt: Resistance of “A2” circuit: less than 1 Ω
Resistance between “A2” circuit and ground: infinity

testgroup.question: Is check result OK?

action.ptxt: Repair or replace defective wire harness.

test1.ptxt: Check starting motor control relay.

testgroup.title: Starting motor power supply circuit check

test1.ptxt: Disconnect starting motor cable.
Check for proper terminal connection to starting motor cable.
If connection is OK, check that voltage between “B1” and ground is battery voltage.

testgroup.question: Is check result OK?

action.ptxt: Repair or replace defective wire harness.

TCM
Mechatronic gear actuator (MGA)

item.ptxt: Limit the driving speed permissible in road conditions and regulations.
DTC P1853 is not detected.
11 V < Battery voltage < 15 V
Transmission warning lamp OFF

s1.ptxt: Select shift selector from N to D range and wait for 5 sec.
Select shift selector from D to R range and wait for 5 sec.
Select shift selector from R to D range and wait for 5 sec.
Select shift selector from D to N range and wait for 5 sec.
With ignition “ON”, leave shift selector for more than 1 minute without operation.
Drive the vehicle at 80km/hr, hold at this speed for 1 min and check 5th gear display in cluster meter.
Stop the vehicle, Check transmission warning lamp status (ON/OFF)
Engage to neutral gear with idling speed.

testgroup.question: Was “Auto Gear Shift System Check” performed?

test1.ptxt: Clear DTC.
Perform “DTC Confirmation Procedure” step 1-10 (~20 cycles)
Check select electro valve current value is more than 0
Check select position value (Difference of Min & Max > 340 bits)
Check respective gears displayed on Instrument cluster
Recheck DTC
Check the selection piston is not move by hand
If selection piston came out from MGA unit
If selection piston stucked / No movement on vertical direction

action.ptxt: Replace MGA unit and recheck DTC.
